mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor demand and consumer income expansion, which drives equity valuations higher through increased corporate earnings expectations and consumer spending. This always-on trend mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 146404.0 thousand as of June 2017 indicates sustained employment growth
failure_condition: Rank IC of PAYEMS momentum signal falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive payroll months do not produce significantly higher forward returns than negative payroll months (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 1.5% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises more than 0.3% month-over-month, the Fed enters a tightening regime that increases equity discount rates and reduces forward earnings multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.04% and CPIAUCSL at 243.79 as of June 2017
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation acceleration
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of cases, or the interaction term (FEDFUNDS × CPIAUCSL momentum) shows Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) declining more than 5% month-over-month signals weakening residential investment and forward consumer confidence, which reduces aggregate demand and equity valuations. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1092.0 thousand units as of May 2017
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce negative 5-day returns in fewer than 52% of cases, or Rank IC of the HOUST decline signal is below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerating above 0.4% month-over-month indicates strengthening manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side productivity gains, which support corporate profit margins and equity valuations. This always-on trend mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC of INDPRO momentum falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or months with INDPRO growth > 0.4% do not produce significantly higher forward returns than months with growth < 0.2% (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) both declining simultaneously signals a demand-side contraction across consumer and residential sectors. The joint deterioration in these two demand proxies predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days due to reduced earnings visibility and multiple compression.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS monthly change < -0.5% AND HOUST monthly change < -3%
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 473513.0 million dollars and HOUST at 1092.0 thousand units as of May-June 2017
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of cases, or the interaction term (RSAFS momentum × HOUST momentum) shows Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising more than 0.4 percentage points over a 3-month period signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer purchasing power, which compresses equity valuations through lower earnings expectations. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: post-shock labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce negative 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of cases, or Rank IC of the UNRATE rise signal is below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-rate environment (FEDFUNDS < 1.5%), inflation measured by CPIAUCSL accelerating above 0.4% month-over-month signals real rate compression and asset price inflation, which drives equity valuations higher through multiple expansion and reduced real discount rates. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.04% indicates low-rate regime as of June 2017
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of cases, or the interaction term shows Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured at quarterly frequency above 2.5% annualized signals robust aggregate demand and corporate earnings expansion, which produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days through higher cash flow expectations and multiple expansion.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 16872.8 billion chained 2017 dollars as of Q1 2017
failure_condition: Rank IC of GDPC1 growth signal falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or quarters with GDPC1 growth > 2.5% do not produce significantly higher forward 60-day returns than quarters with growth < 2.0% (t-test p > 0.10).
